decisions.description: Build a webhook delivery provider by extending AbstractNotificationProviderService with an HTTP-POST provider, packaged as a standalone module rather than part of the notification module.
Initial supported order events are order.placed, order.completed, order.canceled, order.refund_created, and order.fulfillment_created.
Create a WebhookEndpoint data model with URL, HMAC secret, subscribed event types, and merchant ownership.
Sign webhook payloads with HMAC SHA-256 using the endpoint's secret as the key.
Webhook payload envelope contains the event name, timestamp, and full order data matching the Admin API shape so merchants do not have to learn a new format.
Include a UUID idempotency key per delivery attempt in the webhook payload so merchants can deduplicate on their side.
Retry failed webhook deliveries with exponential backoff starting at 30 seconds, capped at 4 hours, for 6 retries total before moving to the dead letter queue.
Implement the dead letter queue as a separate Redis stream and expose an admin endpoint for merchants to view failed deliveries and trigger manual retries.
Apply a per-endpoint token bucket rate limiter at 10 requests per second; overflow requests are queued rather than dropped.
Expose Admin API CRUD routes for webhook endpoints plus a test ping endpoint so merchants can verify endpoints before subscribing to real events.
Emit webhook.delivered and webhook.failed events through EventBusService so operators can build monitoring dashboards.
Bind a workflow to each order event; its step calls the webhook provider which looks up registered endpoints per merchant and POSTs the payload to each matching endpoint.

action_items.text: Draft the module structure and data model by Wednesday
Write the workflow definitions for each order event
Wire up monitoring for webhook.delivered / webhook.failed
